sections.paragraph:
ὁμοιοτρόπως adv. of ὁμοιότροπος ‘of like manner’ (Thu. 6, 20, 3; Aristot., Gen. An. 3, 5; Philo, Aet. M. 20 [all three w. dat.]) in the same way ὁμ. τοῖς προειρημένοις in the same way as those already mentioned Dg 3:2.—DELG s.v. ὅμοιο.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

dat. dat. = dative

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 707
